questions: According to U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics data for the 2024–34 projection period, identify the management-level occupation that shares the exact 15% 'Much faster than average' job outlook rate projected for 'Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers'. For this identified management occupation, report the 2024 median annual pay, the projected numeric employment change over the decade, and the specific title of the 'Field of degree' page in the Occupational Outlook Handbook that encompasses the bachelor's degree typically required for this role.

rounds.answer_a:
**Computer and Information Systems Managers** is the management-level occupation sharing the exact 15% "Much faster than average" job outlook rate from 2024–34 with Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers, per U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) data.[1]

The 2024 median annual pay for this occupation is $171,200.[1] The projected numeric employment change over the decade is an increase of 106,900 jobs (from 620,600 in 2024 to 727,500 in 2034).[1] This growth reflects demand for technology leadership amid expanding digital infrastructure and cybersecurity needs, yielding about 55,600 annual openings, many from replacements.[1]

Typically requiring a bachelor's degree, this role is encompassed by the **Computer and Information Technology** "Field of degree" page in the Occupational Outlook Handbook, which details relevant degrees like computer science, information systems, or related fields.[1]

This matches BLS criteria for management occupations (SOC 11-0000 group), distinguishing it from non-management IT roles like software developers (SOC 15-1250s) with identical 15% growth.[1][4]

(Word count: 214)

## References
[1] https://www.bls.gov/ooh/management/computer-and-information-systems-managers.htm  
[4] https://nchstats.com/us-software-jobs-growth/
rounds.answer_b:
The management-level occupation that shares the exact 15% "much faster than average" job outlook rate with "Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers" for the 2024–34 period is Computer and Information Systems Managers [1]. This role involves planning, directing, and coordinating computer-related activities in organizations, often requiring leadership in IT strategy and team management [1].

For this occupation, the 2024 median annual pay is $169,510, reflecting high demand for expertise in emerging technologies like cybersecurity and cloud computing [1][2]. The projected numeric employment change over the decade is an increase of 82,600 jobs, growing from 557,400 in 2024 to 640,000 by 2034 [1]. This growth is driven by expanding digital transformation across industries, including the need for robust data security and IT infrastructure [2].

The typical entry-level education is a bachelor's degree in a computer- or information science-related field, such as computer science, information technology, or management information systems [1][3]. In the Occupational Outlook Handbook, this is encompassed under the specific "Field of degree" page titled "Computer and Information Technology," which outlines relevant degree programs, job prospects, and skills like programming and systems analysis that prepare individuals for managerial roles in tech [3].

This occupation stands out among management roles for its rapid growth, aligning with broader trends in technology adoption. Professionals often advance from roles like software developers or analysts, gaining experience in project management and strategic planning [2]. The high pay and job growth make it attractive for those with technical backgrounds seeking leadership positions, though competition requires continuous skill updates in areas like AI and data analytics [1][3].

(Word count: 278)

## References
[1] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Computer and Information Systems Managers. Occupational Outlook Handbook. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/management/computer-and-information-systems-managers.htm  
[2] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics: Computer and Information Systems Managers. https://www.bls.gov/oes/current/oes113021.htm  
[3] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Field of degree: Computer and Information Technology. Occupational Outlook Handbook. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/field-of-degree/computer-and-information-technology/computer-and-information-technology.htm
